page: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the Edsv6 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. Additionally, the server includes 2835 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not bundle any public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance across multiple domains. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8), contribute to strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic performance. In Geekbench testing, the instance achieved top-tier results in image processing and strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing. Large language model (LLM) inference speeds are also notable, with top-tier text generation performance on small models and strong prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models. However, Passmark memory benchmarks indicate a weaker relative performance in certain memory-intensive operations, despite strong cached small-block memory read bandwidth.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48ds_v6 offers a high density of memory and local storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring significant memory capacity without the overhead of GPU hardware. The combination of dedicated Intel Xeon cores, substantial local storage, and high memory capacity makes this instance well-suited for memory-intensive applications. Appropriate workloads include relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores), 384.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage. The instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which drive strong benchmark performance in multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and image processing. LLM inference benchmarks show strong to top-tier performance for small and medium models. While Passmark memory scores are relatively weak, cached memory read bandwidth is strong. This high-density memory configuration, combined with substantial local storage and no bundled GPUs, offers a cost-effective profile for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-capacity local storage for demanding database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory and 2835 GB of local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and AI workloads
Strong performance in multi-threaded decompression and image processing benchmarks
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics
